pattern: Stability only ever looks one step to the left, so a single left-to-right scan comparing each element to its immediate predecessor answers the whole question.
hints: Mountain 0 never qualifies, so you only ever need to look at indices 1 and onward.
For each later index, the only thing that matters is the height of the mountain directly before it, not anything further back.
Collect every index i (i >= 1) where height[i - 1] is strictly greater than threshold; the order you collect them in does not matter.

edge_inputs.description: threshold equal to the tallest mountain, nothing qualifies
predecessor height exactly equals the threshold, must not count (strict inequality)
first mountain is tall but index 0 itself can never be stable

stress.description: random small heights and threshold at the constraint ceiling
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    height = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    threshold = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [height, threshold]
